codeReview.summary: The fix restores Central reloads and keeps the live Claude/platform rescan sequencing intact, but it still does not make the global command truly rescan Discover. The action remains labeled as a full rescan while Discover only reloads previously persisted results from the database.

issues.description: The new global rescan path awaits `platformStore.rescan()` and then calls `discoverStore.refreshCounts()` (`src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx:34-39`), but `refreshCounts()` only re-reads persisted Discover rows via `get_discovered_skills` (`src/stores/discoverStore.ts:269-287`). The actual Discover filesystem rescan path is still `startScan()` / `start_project_scan` (`src/stores/discoverStore.ts:201-235`), so new or removed project skills on disk will remain stale after invoking the command-palette action. That makes the command's current label misleading (`src/components/layout/GlobalSearchDialog.tsx:201-212`; `src/i18n/locales/en.json:327`), and the added tests only prove the cached Discover store reloads after platform rescan rather than proving Discover's scan path runs (`src/test/AppShell.test.tsx:139-250`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend worker skill says `.factory/library/user-testing.md` is the canonical native Tauri recipe, but the shared guidance still does not document a reproducible command-palette/global-search automation path. Workers are still rediscovering ad hoc macOS accessibility steps for this surface.

summary: Fail: the fix keeps the live Claude/platform refresh behavior and reloads Central, but the global `Rescan all skills` action still does not rescan Discover from disk, so the original broader-surface regression is only partially fixed.
